greeting: Ask in plain English — install, what KnoSky is, privacy, packs, L3. Short answers first. You can ask for more detail anytime.
fallback:
I only know the published KnoSky FAQ. Try: install, what is KnoSky, what’s in the package, Mode A vs B, which tools, privacy, doctor, or L3 swarm.

Docs: https://knosky.wiki/ · Install: https://www.knosky.com/install.html
more_detail_prompt: Want a bit more technical detail?

entries.title: How to install
entries.simple:
Open a terminal in **your project folder** and run:

`npx knosky@latest .`

That builds a **local map** of your files (and a city view you can open). No account. Your code stays on your machine by default.

You need **Node 20+** installed first.
entries.technical: Under the hood this runs the KnoSky CLI from npm (`knosky@latest`), indexes the folder into `.knosky/city-data.json`, can emit city HTML, and prints MCP config so an assistant can call map/route tools. Optional next step: connect one pack (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or Hermes), then `npx knosky@latest doctor` to see what this install can honestly claim.

entries.title: What’s in the package
entries.simple:
When you install KnoSky you get tools to:

1. **Map** your repo/docs on your machine
2. **Show a city view** people can browse
3. **Help AI assistants find the right files** (instead of guessing)
4. **Optionally prove** access decisions (allow/deny style receipts)
5. **Connect popular assistants** (Claude, Cursor, Codex, Hermes)
6. **Check honesty** with a doctor command
7. **Coordinate several agents carefully** (early “L3” foundation — not a magic swarm product)
8. **Optional PR comments** that suggest where to look (never blocks your build by default)

**Not included:** a coding AI, a cloud vault of your source, or “safe for every swarm everywhere” marketing.
entries.technical: Wave 1 open-source surface: fs indexer + city HTML skin; MCP server with Tier 0 map tools and Tier 1 governed route tools; Mode A advisory vs Mode B lease+policy+audit; P0 packs + P1 stubs; `knosky doctor` claim ceiling; local L3 coordinator (leases/claims/quotas/heatmap/bench); PR-GPS GitHub Action; SSOT docs (tool menu, decision codes, L0–L3 ladder).

entries.title: What is KnoSky?
entries.simple:
KnoSky is **GPS for AI assistants** working in big code and doc folders.

Coding tools can write — they still get lost. KnoSky gives them a **map** and a **route to the right file**. People can look at the same map as a **city view**. It runs on **your computer** by default.
entries.technical: Product category = agent GPS / route governance, not code-intelligence or an IDE. City isometric view is human skin only. Optional Mode B adds identity + policy + audit receipts on governed tools. Public claims must stay ≤ `knosky doctor` for that install.

entries.title: What it is not
entries.simple:
KnoSky is **not** a replacement for Claude, Cursor, or other coding tools.

It does **not** try to be the brain that writes all your code. It helps those tools **navigate** your project — and can optionally record clearer yes/no access decisions. It is also **not** “upload everything to our cloud” in the default path.
entries.technical: Not a coding model/IDE; not a Sourcegraph-style code-intel clone; not Wave-1 multi-tenant SaaS body-store of customer source; not a blanket production swarm-safe fleet claim. Complements MCP hosts via a frozen tool menu (DEC-108).

entries.title: Mode A vs Mode B
entries.simple:
Think of two speeds:

- **Mode A** = helpful **tips only**. “You might look here.” Not a formal yes/no.
- **Mode B** = **checked answers**. The assistant must show who it is, rules are checked, and you get a clear **allow** or **deny** (with a receipt when allowed).

Most day-to-day coding setups can use Mode B when you want real guardrails.
entries.technical: Mode A labels `ADVISORY_UNAUTH`. Mode B returns closed codes like `ALLOW` / `DENY_*` with audit on ALLOW. Identity is a **leaseId** from `npx knosky@latest agent-register ...` — bare agent names are not enough. Dual-operator quorum is required for elevated class registration; operator lease revoke is single-operator today (documented accepted risk).

entries.title: Which tools work?
entries.simple:
KnoSky is built to ride with tools people already use:

- **Claude Code**
- **Cursor**
- **Codex**
- **Hermes**

After you install and build a map, you connect KnoSky once so those assistants can ask for routes. Other paths (like VS Code or PR comments) exist as lighter options.
entries.technical: P0 packs share the DEC-108 MCP menu. Typical env: absolute `KC_CITY` → city-data.json, `KC_PROFILE=coding`, command `node …/mcp/server.mjs`. P1 includes VS Code stub, Greptile recipe, GHA PR-GPS. Coding packs intentionally omit audit tools.

entries.title: Is my code uploaded?
entries.simple:
**Default answer: no.** KnoSky is built to run on **your machine**. The normal install path does **not** send your source code up to KnoSky as a cloud product.

You choose if/when you share a map or city file with someone else. Treat those like a directory listing with short snippets — review before you send.
entries.technical: Local-first / no always-on product telemetry in core. Share-safe indexer can fail closed on secret-like values. Site links are documentation only — default CLI/MCP do not call knosky.com as runtime egress. Windows network lockdown may still warn as unsupported until packaging proves it. See doctor + SECURITY/PRIVACY docs.

entries.title: What is the city map?
entries.simple:
The **city** is a friendly picture of your project for **people** — folders as places, files as buildings — so you can look around.

AI assistants care more about **routes to the right file**. The city is the human skin of the same local map. Build it from **your** code with install — not a fake marketing skyline.
entries.technical: City HTML is generated from the local index; regenerative skin only. Product hero metaphor is GPS routing, not urban skyline. Install: `npx knosky@latest .` in your repo.

entries.title: What is L3 swarm?
entries.simple:
**In plain English:** L3 is the piece of KnoSky that helps **several AI agents work in the same project without stepping on each other as badly** — like taking turns on files and keeping a simple scoreboard.

**Important:** this is an **early foundation**, not a finished “always safe multi-agent fleet for every company” product. Do not read L3 as “turn on workers and forget about risk.”

If you only want map + routing today, you can ignore L3.
entries.technical: Local trust-domain coordinator in `swarm-coordinator`: multi-agent leases, file/district claims, quotas/backpressure, FIFO wait, anti-probe, multi-agent audit meta, heatmap, `knosky swarm status` / `swarm bench`. Not multi-tenant cloud isolation. Doctor reports L3 as FOUNDATION. Operator lease revoke is single-operator (not dual quorum) — accepted risk in SECURITY.md. Bench only on throwaway domains. Details: ladder wiki + GitHub README/SECURITY.

entries.title: What is doctor?
entries.simple:
`doctor` is a **health check** for your install. It tells you what is working and what you should **not** over-claim in public.

Run:

`npx knosky@latest doctor`
entries.technical: Scorecard covers Mode B wiring, packs, ladder L0–L3 honesty, L3 foundation presence, Windows lockdown realism, and similar install ceilings. Marketing copy must stay ≤ doctor for that domain.

entries.title: Does it actually help?
entries.simple:
In KnoSky’s own small test (SAT-439, 5 tasks), a guided path used about **68% fewer tokens**, **70% fewer tool calls**, and reached the right file about **6× faster** than a naive wander.

That’s one first-party study — useful signal, not a promise for every project forever.
entries.technical: First-party harness vs naive agent; methodology in repo/wiki. Do not mix with industry blogs (e.g. GitHub agentic token-efficiency posts) as if those were KnoSky scores — those are problem proof only.

entries.title: What can the assistant ask KnoSky?
entries.simple:
Once connected, an assistant can basically ask KnoSky things like:

- **Find** something in the map
- **Route me** to the right place
- **Check** if an action is allowed (when Mode B is on)

You don’t need the internal names day to day.
entries.technical: Tier 0: `kc_search`, `kc_get_node`, `kc_list_categories`, `kc_get_provenance`, `kc_related`. Tier 1: `kc_route` (dual-mode), `kc_bundle`, `kc_policy_check`. Security profile only: `kc_audit_query`, `kc_audit_verify`. Closed decision codes include ALLOW, DENY_*, ADVISORY_UNAUTH, ERROR_*.

entries.title: Is it free?
entries.simple:
Yes for the core open-source protocol. Run it locally with `npx knosky@latest`. No account wall for the default path.

License is **FSL-1.1-MIT** (free to use with rules against simply reselling a competing hosted copy; converts toward MIT later).
entries.technical: Functional Source License FSL-1.1-MIT details in LICENSE.md on GitHub. npm package `knosky` latest tracks mainline releases (currently 0.7.x line).

entries.title: Where are the docs?
entries.simple:
- **Product marketing:** knosky.com
- **How-to docs:** knosky.wiki
- **Source + deep README:** github.com/SathiaAI/knosky

Start with **Install** if you want hands-on, or **Start here** on the wiki for a short guided path.
entries.technical: Marketing multipage + wiki adoption kit (packs, protocol, ladder, trust). Agent-readable llms.txt on the marketing host.
